title: Updates on Ai Forex trading bot
author: Pale-Cap1820
selftext:
Hello everyone — I wanted to make a post sharing my experience with OpenClaw and trying to automate trading. I figured I’d put it out there in case anyone is interested in hearing how the journey has gone so far.

I’ve always wanted to be a day trader, but I run into the same problem a lot of people do: I have human emotions, and I don’t have unlimited time to sit around analyzing charts all day.

So when OpenClaw started blowing up, I thought, why not see if I can build a group of sub-agents to trade meme coins for me on paper? Surprisingly, it actually worked pretty well at first. For a few days, I was seeing overnight gains of more than 100%.

That only lasted about four days before things started getting buggy. My agents began messing up the Discord displays, the system got unstable, and the costs started climbing fast. At that point, I decided to fully reset everything and try a different approach: sniping earlier launches instead of trading higher market cap coins.

That setup did okay for a bit too, but the sniper bot itself was honestly pretty rough. Then again, I’m self-taught and learning all of this as I go, using ChatGPT and OpenClaw side by side to troubleshoot, build, and figure things out.

After a few more days, the same thing started happening again: bugs, instability, and costs piling up. I was spending around $20 a day just trying to manage everything and keep it from spiraling.

One thing I’ve learned that might help anyone just getting started: have a real plan before you start throwing a bunch of ideas at the wall. Creating and constantly rebuilding systems costs way more than building one solid thing, letting it run, and improving it gradually.

After that, I decided to wipe everything again and shift away from crypto trading. I wanted to focus more on day trading in a market with less volatility and a more controlled environment, where algorithms can actually matter more consistently.

Since then, I’ve built a strategy I’m running now that’s testing around a 68–70% win rate, with about 50–60 trades per month and an average return of around 1.7–1.8R. I also figured out what I needed to connect it to a funded account, and right now I’m debugging that setup day by day.

Yesterday, it placed its first-ever paper trade on a practice funded account, which was a huge milestone for me.

Today was a little rough — I spent about two hours debugging during the Japan open session, so of course I missed the actual trades while I was fixing things. But that’s part of the process, and overall, we’re making progress.

I mostly wanted to share this to document the journey and maybe inspire some of you who are experimenting with this stuff too. It hasn’t been smooth, but I’m learning a lot, and it’s been exciting seeing the progress little by little.

Thanks for reading, and if people are interested, I’ll keep posting updates. Cheers. (Not financial advice)
